verses.context_note: Solomon's wisdom collection; practical instruction for daily living in ancient Israel.
verses.kjv: A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger.
verses.esv: A soft answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.

verses.context_note: Written by James, the brother of Jesus, to the twelve tribes scattered abroad.
verses.kjv: Wherefore, my beloved brethren, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, slow to wrath: For the wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God.
verses.esv: Know this, my beloved brothers: let every person be quick to hear, slow to speak, slow to anger; for the anger of man does not produce the righteousness of God.

verses.context_note: Paul's letter to the church in Ephesus, written during his imprisonment in Rome.
verses.kjv: Be ye angry, and sin not: let not the sun go down upon your wrath: Neither give place to the devil.
verses.esv: Be angry and do not sin; do not let the sun go down on your anger, and give no opportunity to the devil.

verses.context_note: From Solomon's proverbs, contrasting the wise and the foolish.
verses.kjv: A fool uttereth all his mind: but a wise man keepeth it in till afterwards.
verses.esv: A fool gives full vent to his spirit, but a wise man quietly holds it back.

verses.context_note: A psalm of David, instructing believers not to fret over the wicked.
verses.kjv: Cease from anger, and forsake wrath: fret not thyself in any wise to do evil.
verses.esv: Refrain from anger, and forsake wrath! Fret not yourself; it tends only to evil.

verses.context_note: Paul writing to the church at Colossae about putting off the old self.
verses.kjv: But now ye also put off all these; anger, wrath, malice, blasphemy, filthy communication out of your mouth.
verses.esv: But now you must put them all away: anger, wrath, malice, slander, and obscene talk from your mouth.

verses.context_note: Solomon's proverbs contrasting patience with a quick temper.
verses.kjv: He that is slow to wrath is of great understanding: but he that is hasty of spirit exalteth folly.
verses.esv: Whoever is slow to anger has great understanding, but he who has a hasty temper exalts folly.

verses.context_note: Written by the Preacher (traditionally Solomon), reflecting on the meaning of life.
verses.kjv: Be not hasty in thy spirit to be angry: for anger resteth in the bosom of fools.
verses.esv: Be not quick in your spirit to become angry, for anger lodges in the heart of fools.

verses.context_note: Solomon contrasts the wrathful and the patient
verses.kjv: A wrathful man stirreth up strife: but he that is slow to anger appeaseth strife.
verses.esv: A hot-tempered man stirs up strife, but he who is slow to anger quiets contention.

verses.context_note: Solomon on the strength of self-control
verses.kjv: He that is slow to anger is better than the mighty; and he that ruleth his spirit than he that taketh a city.
verses.esv: Whoever is slow to anger is better than the mighty, and he who rules his spirit than he who takes a city.

verses.context_note: Jesus teaching in the Sermon on the Mount
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That whosoever is angry with his brother without a cause shall be in danger of the judgment: and whosoever shall say to his brother, Raca, shall be in danger of the council: but whosoever shall say, Thou fool, shall be in danger of hell fire.
verses.esv: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with his brother will be liable to judgment; whoever insults his brother will be liable to the council; and whoever says, 'You fool!' will be liable to the hell of fire.

verses.context_note: Paul writing to the church in Rome about leaving vengeance to God
verses.kjv: Dearly beloved, avenge not yourselves, but rather give place unto wrath: for it is written, Vengeance is mine; I will repay, saith the Lord.
verses.esv: Beloved, never avenge yourselves, but leave it to the wrath of God, for it is written, "Vengeance is mine, I will repay, says the Lord."

verses.context_note: Solomon warns against keeping company with the angry
verses.kjv: Make no friendship with an angry man; and with a furious man thou shalt not go: Lest thou learn his ways, and get a snare to thy soul.
verses.esv: Make no friendship with a man given to anger, nor go with a wrathful man, lest you learn his ways and entangle yourself in a snare.

verses.context_note: Paul instructing the church in Ephesus to put away bitterness
verses.kjv: Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice:
verses.esv: Let all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamor and slander be put away from you, along with all malice.

verses.context_note: Solomon on the glory of overlooking an offense
verses.kjv: The discretion of a man deferreth his anger; and it is his glory to pass over a transgression.
verses.esv: Good sense makes one slow to anger, and it is his glory to overlook an offense.

verses.context_note: Jesus teaching during the Sermon on the Mount, redefining the law's standard.
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That whosoever looketh on a woman to lust after her hath committed adultery with her already in his heart.
verses.esv: But I say to you that everyone who looks at a woman with lustful intent has already committed adultery with her in his heart.

verses.context_note: Paul writing to the church in Corinth, a city known for its immorality.
verses.kjv: Flee fornication. Every sin that a man doeth is without the body; but he that committeth fornication sinneth against his own body. What? know ye not that your body is the temple of the Holy Ghost which is in you, which ye have of God, and ye are not your own? For ye are bought with a price: therefore glorify God in your body, and in your spirit, which are God's.
verses.esv: Flee from sexual immorality. Every other sin a person commits is outside the body, but the sexually immoral person sins against his own body. Or do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit within you, whom you have from God? You are not your own, for you were bought with a price. So glorify God in your body.

verses.context_note: The apostle John writing to believers about the nature of worldly temptation.
verses.kjv: For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world.
verses.esv: For all that is in the world—the desires of the flesh and the desires of the eyes and pride of life—is not from the Father but is from the world.

verses.context_note: Paul's letter to the churches of Galatia about living by the Spirit.
verses.kjv: This I say then, Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh.
verses.esv: But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh.

verses.context_note: Job's declaration of integrity during his suffering; one of the oldest books of the Bible.
verses.kjv: I made a covenant with mine eyes; why then should I think upon a maid?
verses.esv: I have made a covenant with my eyes; how then could I gaze at a virgin?

verses.context_note: Paul's final letter to his young protege Timothy, written from prison in Rome.
verses.kjv: Flee also youthful lusts: but follow righteousness, faith, charity, peace, with them that call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
verses.esv: So flee youthful passions and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace, along with those who call on the Lord from a pure heart.

verses.context_note: Paul's letter to the church in Rome, instructing believers on holy living.
verses.kjv: But put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the lusts thereof.
verses.esv: But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, to gratify its desires.

verses.context_note: Paul writing to the church in Thessalonica about sanctification.
verses.kjv: For this is the will of God, even your sanctification, that ye should abstain from fornication: That every one of you should know how to possess his vessel in sanctification and honour; Not in the lust of concupiscence, even as the Gentiles which know not God:
verses.esv: For this is the will of God, your sanctification: that you abstain from sexual immorality; that each one of you know how to control his own body in holiness and honor, not in the passion of lust like the Gentiles who do not know God;

verses.context_note: Solomon warns against the allure of the adulterous
verses.kjv: Lust not after her beauty in thine heart; neither let her take thee with her eyelids.
verses.esv: Do not desire her beauty in your heart, and do not let her capture you with her eyelashes.

verses.context_note: James, brother of Jesus, on the progression of temptation
verses.kjv: But every man is tempted, when he is drawn away of his own lust, and enticed. Then when lust hath conceived, it bringeth forth sin: and sin, when it is finished, bringeth forth death.
verses.esv: But each person is tempted when he is lured and enticed by his own desire. Then desire when it has conceived gives birth to sin, and sin when it is fully grown brings forth death.

verses.context_note: Paul urging the church at Colossae to put to death earthly desires
verses.kjv: Mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth; fornication, uncleanness, inordinate affection, evil concupiscence, and covetousness, which is idolatry:
verses.esv: Put to death therefore what is earthly in you: sexual immorality, impurity, passion, evil desire, and covetousness, which is idolatry.

verses.context_note: Solomon on guarding the heart as the source of life
verses.kjv: Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life.
verses.esv: Keep your heart with all vigilance, for from it flow the springs of life.

verses.context_note: Peter writing to the scattered believers about abstaining from fleshly lusts
verses.kjv: Dearly beloved, I beseech you as strangers and pilgrims, abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul;
verses.esv: Beloved, I urge you as sojourners and exiles to abstain from the passions of the flesh, which wage war against your soul.

verses.context_note: The psalmist on keeping one's way pure through God's word
verses.kjv: Wherewithal shall a young man cleanse his way? by taking heed thereto according to thy word. With my whole heart have I sought thee: O let me not wander from thy commandments. Thy word have I hid in mine heart, that I might not sin against thee.
verses.esv: How can a young man keep his way pure? By guarding it according to your word. With my whole heart I seek you; let me not wander from your commandments! I have stored up your word in my heart, that I might not sin against you.

verses.context_note: Solomon's proverbs revealing God's view on honesty and deception.
verses.kjv: Lying lips are abomination to the LORD: but they that deal truly are his delight.
verses.esv: Lying lips are an abomination to the LORD, but those who act faithfully are his delight.

verses.context_note: Paul urging the Colossian believers to live as new creations in Christ.
verses.kjv: Lie not one to another, seeing that ye have put off the old man with his deeds; And have put on the new man, which is renewed in knowledge after the image of him that created him:
verses.esv: Do not lie to one another, seeing that you have put off the old self with its practices and have put on the new self, which is being renewed in knowledge after the image of its creator.

verses.context_note: Paul writing to the Ephesians about living as members of one body in Christ.
verses.kjv: Wherefore putting away lying, speak every man truth with his neighbour: for we are members one of another.
verses.esv: Therefore, having put away falsehood, let each one of you speak the truth with his neighbor, for we are members one of another.

verses.context_note: Jesus confronting the Pharisees in the temple courts, identifying the origin of lies.
verses.kjv: Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do. He was a murderer from the beginning, and abode not in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own: for he is a liar, and the father of it.
verses.esv: You are of your father the devil, and your will is to do your father's desires. He was a murderer from the beginning, and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks out of his own character, for he is a liar and the father of lies.

verses.context_note: A psalm of David declaring his commitment to integrity in his household.
verses.kjv: He that worketh deceit shall not dwell within my house: he that telleth lies shall not tarry in my sight.
verses.esv: No one who practices deceit shall dwell in my house; no one who utters lies shall continue before my eyes.

verses.context_note: Solomon's wisdom about the consequences of bearing false witness.
verses.kjv: A false witness shall not be unpunished, and he that speaketh lies shall perish.
verses.esv: A false witness will not go unpunished, and he who breathes out lies will perish.

verses.context_note: The prophet Zechariah delivering God's instruction to the returned exiles in Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: These are the things that ye shall do; Speak ye every man the truth to his neighbour; execute the judgment of truth and peace in your gates: And let none of you imagine evil in your hearts against his neighbour; and love no false oath: for all these are things that I hate, saith the LORD.
verses.esv: These are the things that you shall do: Speak the truth to one another; render in your gates judgments that are true and make for peace; do not devise evil in your hearts against one another, and love no false oath, for all these things I hate, declares the LORD.

verses.context_note: The apostle John's vision of the final judgment, listing those excluded from the new creation.
verses.kjv: But the fearful, and unbelieving, and the abominable, and murderers, and whoremongers, and sorcerers, and idolaters, and all liars, shall have their part in the lake which burneth with fire and brimstone: which is the second death.
verses.esv: But as for the cowardly, the faithless, the detestable, as for murderers, the sexually immoral, sorcerers, idolaters, and all liars, their portion will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur, which is the second death.

verses.context_note: Solomon listing the things the Lord hates
verses.kjv: These six things doth the LORD hate: yea, seven are an abomination unto him: A proud look, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood, An heart that deviseth wicked imaginations, feet that be swift in running to mischief, A false witness that speaketh lies, and he that soweth discord among brethren.
verses.esv: There are six things that the LORD hates, seven that are an abomination to him: haughty eyes, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood, a heart that devises wicked plans, feet that make haste to run to evil, a false witness who breathes out lies, and one who sows discord among brothers.

verses.context_note: David instructing on keeping the tongue from evil
verses.kjv: Keep thy tongue from evil, and thy lips from speaking guile.
verses.esv: Keep your tongue from evil and your lips from speaking deceit.

verses.context_note: The ninth commandment given by God to Moses at Sinai
verses.kjv: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.
verses.esv: You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.

verses.context_note: Solomon on integrity versus deception
verses.kjv: The integrity of the upright shall guide them: but the perverseness of transgressors shall destroy them.
verses.esv: The integrity of the upright guides them, but the crookedness of the treacherous destroys them.

verses.context_note: God commanding Moses regarding honesty among the people
verses.kjv: Ye shall not steal, neither deal falsely, neither lie one to another.
verses.esv: You shall not steal; you shall not deal falsely; you shall not lie to one another.

verses.context_note: Solomon on the destructive nature of a lying tongue
verses.kjv: A lying tongue hateth those that are afflicted by it; and a flattering mouth worketh ruin.
verses.esv: A lying tongue hates its victims, and a flattering mouth works ruin.

verses.context_note: Solomon's wisdom on the destructive nature of envy.
verses.kjv: A sound heart is the life of the flesh: but envy the rottenness of the bones.
verses.esv: A tranquil heart gives life to the flesh, but envy makes the bones rot.

verses.context_note: Paul warning the churches of Galatia against vain conceit and envy.
verses.kjv: Let us not be desirous of vain glory, provoking one another, envying one another.
verses.esv: Let us not become conceited, provoking one another, envying one another.

verses.context_note: James, the brother of Jesus, warning about the fruit of jealousy.
verses.kjv: For where envying and strife is, there is confusion and every evil work.
verses.esv: For where jealousy and selfish ambition exist, there will be disorder and every vile practice.

verses.context_note: Paul's famous description of love in his letter to the church in Corinth.
verses.kjv: Charity suffereth long, and is kind; charity envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up,
verses.esv: Love is patient and kind; love does not envy or boast; it is not arrogant or rude.

verses.context_note: A poetic celebration of love attributed to Solomon, revealing jealousy's fierce nature.
verses.kjv: Set me as a seal upon thine heart, as a seal upon thine arm: for love is strong as death; jealousy is cruel as the grave: the coals thereof are coals of fire, which hath a most vehement flame.
verses.esv: Set me as a seal upon your heart, as a seal upon your arm, for love is strong as death, jealousy is fierce as the grave. Its flashes are flashes of fire, the very flame of the LORD.

verses.context_note: Solomon's proverbs comparing wrath, anger, and envy.
verses.kjv: Wrath is cruel, and anger is outrageous; but who is able to stand before envy?
verses.esv: Wrath is cruel, anger is overwhelming, but who can stand before jealousy?

verses.context_note: Paul writing from prison to the church at Philippi about humility.
verses.kjv: Let nothing be done through strife or vainglory; but in lowliness of mind let each esteem other better than themselves.
verses.esv: Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit, but in humility count others more significant than yourselves.

verses.context_note: Paul urging the Roman believers to walk properly as in the daytime.
verses.kjv: Let us walk honestly, as in the day; not in rioting and drunkenness, not in chambering and wantonness, not in strife and envying.
verses.esv: Let us walk properly as in the daytime, not in orgies and drunkenness, not in sexual immorality and sensuality, not in quarreling and jealousy.

verses.context_note: Solomon warning against envying sinners
verses.kjv: Let not thine heart envy sinners: but be thou in the fear of the LORD all the day long.
verses.esv: Let not your heart envy sinners, but continue in the fear of the LORD all the day.

verses.context_note: James on the source of bitter jealousy
verses.kjv: But if ye have bitter envying and strife in your hearts, glory not, and lie not against the truth. This wisdom descendeth not from above, but is earthly, sensual, devilish.
verses.esv: But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your hearts, do not boast and be false to the truth. This is not the wisdom that comes down from above, but is earthly, unspiritual, demonic.

verses.context_note: David instructing against envying evildoers
verses.kjv: Fret not thyself because of evildoers, neither be thou envious against the workers of iniquity.
verses.esv: Fret not yourself because of evildoers; be not envious of wrongdoers!

verses.context_note: Solomon observing that toil is driven by envy
verses.kjv: Again, I considered all travail, and every right work, that for this a man is envied of his neighbour. This is also vanity and vexation of spirit.
verses.esv: Then I saw that all toil and all skill in work come from a man's envy of his neighbor. This also is vanity and a striving after wind.

verses.context_note: Paul listing the works of the flesh to the churches of Galatia
verses.kjv: Now the works of the flesh are manifest, which are these; Adultery, fornication, uncleanness, lasciviousness, Idolatry, witchcraft, hatred, variance, emulations, wrath, strife, seditions, heresies, Envyings, murders, drunkenness, revellings, and such like: of the which I tell you before, as I have also told you in time past, that they which do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of God.
verses.esv: Now the works of the flesh are evident: sexual immorality, impurity, sensuality, idolatry, sorcery, enmity, strife, jealousy, fits of anger, rivalries, dissensions, divisions, envy, drunkenness, orgies, and things like these. I warn you, as I warned you before, that those who do such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.

verses.context_note: God's command to Joshua as he prepares to lead Israel into the Promised Land after Moses' death.
verses.kjv: Have not I commanded thee? Be strong and of a good courage; be not afraid, neither be thou dismayed: for the LORD thy God is with thee whithersoever thou goest.
verses.esv: Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be frightened, and do not be dismayed, for the LORD your God is with you wherever you go.

verses.context_note: The prophet Isaiah delivering God's reassurance to Israel during the threat of exile.
verses.kjv: Fear thou not; for I am with thee: be not dismayed; for I am thy God: I will strengthen thee; yea, I will help thee; yea, I will uphold thee with the right hand of my righteousness.
verses.esv: Fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my righteous right hand.

verses.context_note: Paul encouraging Timothy not to be timid in his ministry, written from a Roman prison.
verses.kjv: For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.
verses.esv: For God gave us a spirit not of fear but of power and love and self-control.

verses.context_note: David's beloved psalm of trust, written from his experience as a shepherd.
verses.kjv: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me; thy rod and thy staff they comfort me.
verses.esv: Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.

verses.context_note: A psalm of David expressing bold confidence in the Lord as his protector.
verses.kjv: The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? the LORD is the strength of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
verses.esv: The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? The LORD is the stronghold of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?

verses.context_note: David's psalm when the Philistines seized him in Gath, choosing trust over fear.
verses.kjv: What time I am afraid, I will trust in thee. In God I will praise his word, in God I have put my trust; I will not fear what flesh can do unto me.
verses.esv: When I am afraid, I put my trust in you. In God, whose word I praise, in God I trust; I shall not be afraid. What can flesh do to me?

verses.context_note: Paul writing to the church in Rome about the believer's adoption as God's children.
verses.kjv: For ye have not received the spirit of bondage again to fear; but ye have received the Spirit of adoption, whereby we cry, Abba, Father.
verses.esv: For you did not receive the spirit of slavery to fall back into fear, but you have received the Spirit of adoption as sons, by whom we cry, "Abba! Father!"

verses.context_note: Moses' final encouragement to Israel before his death, as they prepare to enter Canaan.
verses.kjv: Be strong and of a good courage, fear not, nor be afraid of them: for the LORD thy God, he it is that doth go with thee; he will not fail thee, nor forsake thee.
verses.esv: Be strong and courageous. Do not fear or be in dread of them, for it is the LORD your God who goes with you. He will not leave you or forsake you.

verses.context_note: David praising God for deliverance from all his fears
verses.kjv: I sought the LORD, and he heard me, and delivered me from all my fears.
verses.esv: I sought the LORD, and he answered me and delivered me from all my fears.

verses.context_note: God speaking through Isaiah to reassure Israel
verses.kjv: But now thus saith the LORD that created thee, O Jacob, and he that formed thee, O Israel, Fear not: for I have redeemed thee, I have called thee by thy name; thou art mine.
verses.esv: But now thus says the LORD, he who created you, O Jacob, he who formed you, O Israel: "Fear not, for I have redeemed you; I have called you by name, you are mine."

verses.context_note: A psalm of trust in God's protection
verses.kjv: He that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty. I will say of the LORD, He is my refuge and my fortress: my God; in him will I trust.
verses.esv: He who dwells in the shelter of the Most High will abide in the shadow of the Almighty. I will say to the LORD, "My refuge and my fortress, my God, in whom I trust."

verses.context_note: A psalm of thanksgiving for God's faithfulness
verses.kjv: The LORD is on my side; I will not fear: what can man do unto me?
verses.esv: The LORD is on my side; I will not fear. What can man do to me?

verses.context_note: The author of Hebrews encouraging confidence in God
verses.kjv: So that we may boldly say, The Lord is my helper, and I will not fear what man shall do unto me.
verses.esv: So we can confidently say, "The Lord is my helper; I will not fear; what can man do to me?"

verses.context_note: Jesus instructing his disciples before sending them out
verses.kjv: And fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.
verses.esv: And do not fear those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul. Rather fear him who can destroy both soul and body in hell.

verses.context_note: Paul writing from prison to the church at Philippi about rejoicing and peace.
verses.kjv: Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God. And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.
verses.esv: Do not be anxious about anything, but in everything by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God. And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.

verses.context_note: Jesus teaching during the Sermon on the Mount about trusting God for provision.
verses.kjv: Therefore I say unto you, Take no thought for your life, what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink; nor yet for your body, what ye shall put on. Is not the life more than meat, and the body than raiment? Behold the fowls of the air: for they sow not, neither do they reap, nor gather into barns; yet your heavenly Father feedeth them. Are ye not much better than they? Which of you by taking thought can add one cubit unto his stature?
verses.esv: Therefore I tell you, do not be anxious about your life, what you will eat or what you will drink, nor about your body, what you will put on. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothing? Look at the birds of the air: they neither sow nor reap nor gather into barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not of more value than they? And which of you by being anxious can add a single hour to his span of life?

verses.context_note: The apostle Peter writing to the persecuted Christians scattered throughout Asia Minor.
verses.kjv: Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.
verses.esv: Casting all your anxieties on him, because he cares for you.

verses.context_note: Isaiah's song of trust in the Lord, looking forward to God's ultimate deliverance.
verses.kjv: Thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee: because he trusteth in thee.
verses.esv: You keep him in perfect peace whose mind is stayed on you, because he trusts in you.

verses.context_note: David's psalm during a time of betrayal by a close friend.
verses.kjv: Cast thy burden upon the LORD, and he shall sustain thee: he shall never suffer the righteous to be moved.
verses.esv: Cast your burden on the LORD, and he will sustain you; he will never permit the righteous to be moved.

verses.context_note: A psalm calling on God as judge, finding comfort amid anxious thoughts.
verses.kjv: In the multitude of my thoughts within me thy comforts delight my soul.
verses.esv: When the cares of my heart are many, your consolations cheer my soul.

verses.context_note: Jesus concluding His teaching on worry in the Sermon on the Mount.
verses.kjv: Take therefore no thought for the morrow: for the morrow shall take thought for the things of itself. Sufficient unto the day is the evil thereof.
verses.esv: Therefore do not be anxious about tomorrow, for tomorrow will be anxious for itself. Sufficient for the day is its own trouble.

verses.context_note: Jesus speaking to His disciples at the Last Supper before His crucifixion.
verses.kjv: Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
verses.esv: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not your hearts be troubled, neither let them be afraid.

verses.context_note: A psalm of confidence in God's presence and power
verses.kjv: God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble. Therefore will not we fear, though the earth be removed, and though the mountains be carried into the midst of the sea;
verses.esv: God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble. Therefore we will not fear though the earth gives way, though the mountains be moved into the heart of the sea,

verses.context_note: Solomon on the weight of anxiety and the power of a good word
verses.kjv: Heaviness in the heart of man maketh it stoop: but a good word maketh it glad.
verses.esv: Anxiety in a man's heart weighs him down, but a good word makes him glad.

verses.context_note: Jesus inviting the weary to find rest in him
verses.kjv: Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls. For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.
verses.esv: Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light."

verses.context_note: David inviting God to search his heart and remove anxiety
verses.kjv: Search me, O God, and know my heart: try me, and know my thoughts: And see if there be any wicked way in me, and lead me in the way everlasting.
verses.esv: Search me, O God, and know my heart! Try me and know my thoughts! And see if there be any grievous way in me, and lead me in the way everlasting!

verses.context_note: Jeremiah finding hope in God's faithfulness amid despair
verses.kjv: It is of the LORD's mercies that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not. They are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness.
verses.esv: The steadfast love of the LORD never ceases; his mercies never come to an end; they are new every morning; great is your faithfulness.

verses.context_note: David's psalm after escaping from King Abimelech by feigning madness.
verses.kjv: The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.
verses.esv: The LORD is near to the brokenhearted and saves the crushed in spirit.

verses.context_note: Jesus delivering the Beatitudes at the opening of the Sermon on the Mount.
verses.kjv: Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.
verses.esv: Blessed are those who mourn, for they shall be comforted.

verses.context_note: John's vision of the new heaven and new earth, where God dwells with His people.
verses.kjv: And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain: for the former things are passed away.
verses.esv: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes, and death shall be no more, neither shall there be mourning, nor crying, nor pain anymore, for the former things have passed away.

verses.context_note: Paul writing to the church in Corinth about the God of all comfort.
verses.kjv: Blessed be God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies, and the God of all comfort; Who comforteth us in all our tribulation, that we may be able to comfort them which are in any trouble, by the comfort wherewith we ourselves are comforted of God.
verses.esv: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort, who comforts us in all our affliction, so that we may be able to comfort those who are in any affliction, with the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.

verses.context_note: A psalm of praise celebrating God's care for the brokenhearted in Israel.
verses.kjv: He healeth the broken in heart, and bindeth up their wounds.
verses.esv: He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.

verses.context_note: Jesus weeping at the tomb of His friend Lazarus, sharing in human grief.
verses.kjv: Jesus wept.
verses.esv: Jesus wept.

verses.context_note: Paul writing to the church in Rome about God's sovereign purpose in suffering.
verses.kjv: And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.
verses.esv: And we know that for those who love God all things work together for good, for those who are called according to his purpose.

verses.context_note: Isaiah's prophecy of the suffering servant, foretelling the Messiah's grief.
verses.kjv: He is despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows, and acquainted with grief: and we hid as it were our faces from him; he was despised, and we esteemed him not. Surely he hath borne our griefs, and carried our sorrows: yet we did esteem him stricken, smitten of God, and afflicted.
verses.esv: He was despised and rejected by men, a man of sorrows and acquainted with grief; and as one from whom men hide their faces he was despised, and we esteemed him not. Surely he has borne our griefs and carried our sorrows; yet we esteemed him stricken, smitten by God, and afflicted.

verses.context_note: David on the brevity of God's anger and the joy that comes
verses.kjv: For his anger endureth but a moment; in his favour is life: weeping may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning.
verses.esv: For his anger is but for a moment, and his favor is for a lifetime. Weeping may tarry for the night, but joy comes with the morning.

verses.context_note: Isaiah prophesying comfort for those who mourn in Zion
verses.kjv: The Spirit of the Lord GOD is upon me; because the LORD hath anointed me to preach good tidings unto the meek; he hath sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives, and the opening of the prison to them that are bound; To proclaim the acceptable year of the LORD, and the day of vengeance of our God; to comfort all that mourn; To appoint unto them that mourn in Zion, to give unto them beauty for ashes, the oil of joy for mourning, the garment of praise for the spirit of heaviness; that they might be called trees of righteousness, the planting of the LORD, that he might be glorified.
verses.esv: The Spirit of the Lord GOD is upon me, because the LORD has anointed me to bring good news to the poor; he has sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives, and the opening of the prison to those who are bound; to proclaim the year of the LORD's favor, and the day of vengeance of our God; to comfort all who mourn; to grant to those who mourn in Zion--to give them a beautiful headdress instead of ashes, the oil of gladness instead of mourning, the garment of praise instead of a faint spirit; that they may be called oaks of righteousness, the planting of the LORD, that he may be glorified.

verses.context_note: Jesus comforting his disciples before his crucifixion
verses.kjv: Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me. In my Father's house are many mansions: if it were not so, I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you. And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you unto myself; that where I am, there ye may be also.
verses.esv: Let not your hearts be troubled. Believe in God; believe also in me. In my Father's house are many rooms. If it were not so, would I have told you that I go to prepare a place for you? And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again and will take you to myself, that where I am you may be also.

verses.context_note: Jeremiah finding hope in God's faithfulness amid sorrow
verses.kjv: It is of the LORD's mercies that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not. They are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness.
verses.esv: The steadfast love of the LORD never ceases; his mercies never come to an end; they are new every morning; great is your faithfulness.

verses.context_note: Solomon's wisdom about the danger of pride and arrogance.
verses.kjv: Pride goeth before destruction, and an haughty spirit before a fall.
verses.esv: Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.

verses.context_note: James quoting Proverbs to show God's stance toward the proud and the humble.
verses.kjv: But he giveth more grace. Wherefore he saith, God resisteth the proud, but giveth grace unto the humble.
verses.esv: But he gives more grace. Therefore it says, "God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble."

verses.context_note: Paul urging the Philippian church to imitate Christ's humility.
verses.kjv: Let nothing be done through strife or vainglory; but in lowliness of mind let each esteem other better than themselves. Look not every man on his own things, but every man also on the things of others.
verses.esv: Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit, but in humility count others more significant than yourselves. Let each of you look not only to his own interests, but also to the interests of others.

verses.context_note: Solomon contrasting the outcome of pride and humility.
verses.kjv: When pride cometh, then cometh shame: but with the lowly is wisdom.
verses.esv: When pride comes, then comes disgrace, but with the humble is wisdom.

verses.context_note: Peter writing to persecuted believers about clothing themselves with humility.
verses.kjv: Likewise, ye younger, submit yourselves unto the elder. Yea, all of you be subject one to another, and be clothed with humility: for God resisteth the proud, and giveth grace to the humble. Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that he may exalt you in due time:
verses.esv: Likewise, you who are younger, be subject to the elders. Clothe yourselves, all of you, with humility toward one another, for "God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble." Humble yourselves, therefore, under the mighty hand of God so that at the proper time he may exalt you,

verses.context_note: Paul instructing the church in Rome on sober self-assessment.
verses.kjv: For I say, through the grace given unto me, to every man that is among you, not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think; but to think soberly, according as God hath dealt to every man the measure of faith.
verses.esv: For by the grace given to me I say to everyone among you not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think, but to think with sober judgment, each according to the measure of faith that God has assigned.

verses.context_note: Solomon's wisdom on self-promotion versus letting others speak well of you.
verses.kjv: Let another man praise thee, and not thine own mouth; a stranger, and not thine own lips.
verses.esv: Let another praise you, and not your own mouth; a stranger, and not your own lips.

verses.context_note: The prophet Micah summarizing what God requires of His people.
verses.kjv: He hath shewed thee, O man, what is good; and what doth the LORD require of thee, but to do justly, and to love mercy, and to walk humbly with thy God?
verses.esv: He has told you, O man, what is good; and what does the LORD require of you but to do justice, and to love kindness, and to walk humbly with your God?

verses.context_note: Wisdom personified declaring what is hateful to God
verses.kjv: The fear of the LORD is to hate evil: pride, and arrogancy, and the evil way, and the froward mouth, do I hate.
verses.esv: The fear of the LORD is hatred of evil. Pride and arrogance and the way of evil and perverted speech I hate.

verses.context_note: Nebuchadnezzar's testimony after God humbled him
verses.kjv: Now I Nebuchadnezzar praise and extol and honour the King of heaven, all whose works are truth, and his ways judgment: and those that walk in pride he is able to abase.
verses.esv: Now I, Nebuchadnezzar, praise and extol and honor the King of heaven, for all his works are right and his ways are just; and those who walk in pride he is able to humble.

verses.context_note: Jesus teaching at a Pharisee's house about humility
verses.kjv: For whosoever exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.
verses.esv: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and he who humbles himself will be exalted.

verses.context_note: Paul warning the churches of Galatia against self-deception
verses.kjv: For if a man think himself to be something, when he is nothing, he deceiveth himself.
verses.esv: For if anyone thinks he is something, when he is nothing, he deceives himself.

verses.context_note: Solomon contrasting pride and humility
verses.kjv: A man's pride shall bring him low: but honour shall uphold the humble in spirit.
verses.esv: One's pride will bring him low, but he who is lowly in spirit will obtain honor.

verses.context_note: Isaiah prophesying the day of the Lord against the proud
verses.kjv: For the day of the LORD of hosts shall be upon every one that is proud and lofty, and upon every one that is lifted up; and he shall be brought low:
verses.esv: For the LORD of hosts has a day against all that is proud and lofty, against all that is lifted up--and it shall be brought low;

verses.context_note: Paul addressing the church in Corinth on freedom in Christ and its limits.
verses.kjv: All things are lawful unto me, but all things are not expedient: all things are lawful for me, but I will not be brought under the power of any.
verses.esv: "All things are lawful for me," but not all things are helpful. "All things are lawful for me," but I will not be dominated by anything.

verses.context_note: Jesus speaking in the temple about true freedom from the bondage of sin.
verses.kjv: If the Son therefore shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed.
verses.esv: So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.

verses.context_note: Paul urging the Galatian believers to stand firm in the freedom Christ provides.
verses.kjv: Stand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage.
verses.esv: For freedom Christ has set us free; stand firm therefore, and do not submit again to a yoke of slavery.

verses.context_note: Paul explaining to the church in Rome that sin no longer has dominion over believers.
verses.kjv: For sin shall not have dominion over you: for ye are not under the law, but under grace.
verses.esv: For sin will have no dominion over you, since you are not under law but under grace.

verses.context_note: Peter warning about false teachers who promise freedom but are themselves enslaved.
verses.kjv: While they promise them liberty, they themselves are the servants of corruption: for of whom a man is overcome, of the same is he brought in bondage.
verses.esv: They promise them freedom, but they themselves are slaves of corruption. For whatever overcomes a person, to that he is enslaved.

verses.context_note: Paul reassuring the Corinthian believers that God provides a way through temptation.
verses.kjv: There hath no temptation taken you but such as is common to man: but God is faithful, who will not suffer you to be tempted above that ye are able; but will with the temptation also make a way to escape, that ye may be able to bear it.
verses.esv: No temptation has overtaken you that is not common to man. God is faithful, and he will not let you be tempted beyond your ability, but with the temptation he will also provide the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.

verses.context_note: Paul urging the Roman believers toward transformation through renewed thinking.
verses.kjv: And be not conformed to this world: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind, that ye may prove what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, will of God.
verses.esv: Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewal of your mind, that by testing you may discern what is the will of God, what is good and acceptable and perfect.

verses.context_note: Isaiah's messianic prophecy about freedom for captives, later quoted by Jesus in Nazareth.
verses.kjv: The Spirit of the Lord GOD is upon me; because the LORD hath anointed me to preach good tidings unto the meek; he hath sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives, and the opening of the prison to them that are bound;
verses.esv: The Spirit of the Lord GOD is upon me, because the LORD has anointed me to bring good news to the poor; he has sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives, and the opening of the prison to those who are bound;

verses.context_note: Paul writing to Titus about the grace of God that teaches self-control
verses.kjv: For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men, Teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world;
verses.esv: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation for all people, training us to renounce ungodliness and worldly passions, and to live self-controlled, upright, and godly lives in the present age,

verses.context_note: Paul writing from prison to the church at Philippi about strength in Christ
verses.kjv: I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.
verses.esv: I can do all things through him who strengthens me.

verses.context_note: A psalm celebrating God's deliverance of those in bondage
verses.kjv: Then they cried unto the LORD in their trouble, and he saved them out of their distresses. He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and brake their bands in sunder.
verses.esv: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress. He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and burst their bonds apart.

verses.context_note: James, brother of Jesus, on resisting the devil
verses.kjv: Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
verses.esv: Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.

verses.context_note: Solomon's vivid warning against the bondage of strong drink
verses.kjv: Who hath woe? who hath sorrow? who hath contentions? who hath babbling? who hath wounds without cause? who hath redness of eyes? They that tarry long at the wine; they that go to seek mixed wine. Look not thou upon the wine when it is red, when it giveth his colour in the cup, when it moveth itself aright. At the last it biteth like a serpent, and stingeth like an adder.
verses.esv: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has strife? Who has complaining? Who has wounds without cause? Who has redness of eyes? Those who tarry long over wine; those who go to try mixed wine. Do not look at wine when it is red, when it sparkles in the cup and goes down smoothly. In the end it bites like a serpent and stings like an adder.

verses.context_note: Peter warning the scattered believers to be vigilant
verses.kjv: Be sober, be vigilant; because your adversary the devil, as a roaring lion, walketh about, seeking whom he may devour:
verses.esv: Be sober-minded; be watchful. Your adversary the devil prowls around like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.

verses.context_note: Jesus teaching on forgiveness immediately following the Lord's Prayer.
verses.kjv: For if ye forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you: But if ye forgive not men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.
verses.esv: For if you forgive others their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you, but if you do not forgive others their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.

verses.context_note: Paul instructing the church in Ephesus to put away bitterness and practice forgiveness.
verses.kjv: Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice: And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ's sake hath forgiven you.
verses.esv: Let all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamor and slander be put away from you, along with all malice. Be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, as God in Christ forgave you.

verses.context_note: Paul urging the Colossian believers to bear with one another as the Lord forgave them.
verses.kjv: Forbearing one another, and forgiving one another, if any man have a quarrel against any: even as Christ forgave you, so also do ye.
verses.esv: Bearing with one another and, if one has a complaint against another, forgiving each other; as the Lord has forgiven you, so you also must forgive.

verses.context_note: Jesus teaching His disciples about prayer and forgiveness near the temple.
verses.kjv: And when ye stand praying, forgive, if ye have ought against any: that your Father also which is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses.
verses.esv: And whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone, so that your Father also who is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses.

verses.context_note: Jesus teaching on the plain about judging, condemning, and forgiving.
verses.kjv: Judge not, and ye shall not be judged: condemn not, and ye shall not be condemned: forgive, and ye shall be forgiven:
verses.esv: Judge not, and you will not be judged; condemn not, and you will not be condemned; forgive, and you will be forgiven;

verses.context_note: Peter asking Jesus how many times to forgive, and Jesus' radical answer.
verses.kjv: Then came Peter to him, and said, Lord, how oft shall my brother sin against me, and I forgive him? till seven times? Jesus saith unto him, I say not unto thee, Until seven times: but, Until seventy times seven.
verses.esv: Then Peter came up and said to him, "Lord, how often will my brother sin against me, and I forgive him? As many as seven times?" Jesus said to him, "I do not say to you seven times, but seventy-seven times.

verses.context_note: Paul writing to the Romans about leaving vengeance to God.
verses.kjv: Dearly beloved, avenge not yourselves, but rather give place unto wrath: for it is written, Vengeance is mine; I will repay, saith the Lord.
verses.esv: Beloved, never avenge yourselves, but leave it to the wrath of God, for it is written, "Vengeance is mine, I will repay, says the Lord."

verses.context_note: The author of Hebrews warning believers against the root of bitterness.
verses.kjv: Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord: Looking diligently lest any man fail of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and thereby many be defiled;
verses.esv: Strive for peace with everyone, and for the holiness without which no one will see the Lord. See to it that no one fails to obtain the grace of God; that no "root of bitterness" springs up and causes trouble, and by it many become defiled;

verses.context_note: Jesus teaching his disciples about repeated forgiveness
verses.kjv: Take heed to yourselves: If thy brother trespass against thee, rebuke him; and if he repent, forgive him. And if he trespass against thee seven times in a day, and seven times in a day turn again to thee, saying, I repent; thou shalt forgive him.
verses.esv: Pay attention to yourselves! If your brother sins, rebuke him, and if he repents, forgive him, and if he sins against you seven times in the day, and turns to you seven times, saying, "I repent," you must forgive him.

verses.context_note: Paul urging forgiveness to prevent Satan's schemes
verses.kjv: To whom ye forgive any thing, I forgive also: for if I forgave any thing, to whom I forgave it, for your sakes forgave I it in the person of Christ; Lest Satan should get an advantage of us: for we are not ignorant of his devices.
verses.esv: Anyone whom you forgive, I also forgive. Indeed, what I have forgiven, if I have forgiven anything, has been for your sake in the presence of Christ, so that we would not be outwitted by Satan; for we are not ignorant of his designs.

verses.context_note: Jesus commanding love for enemies in the Sermon on the Mount
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully use you, and persecute you;
verses.esv: But I say to you, Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,

verses.context_note: Solomon on the glory of passing over offenses
verses.kjv: The discretion of a man deferreth his anger; and it is his glory to pass over a transgression.
verses.esv: Good sense makes one slow to anger, and it is his glory to overlook an offense.

verses.context_note: A father's honest cry to Jesus when seeking healing for his demon-possessed son.
verses.kjv: And straightway the father of the child cried out, and said with tears, Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief.
verses.esv: Immediately the father of the child cried out and said, "I believe; help my unbelief!"

verses.context_note: The author of Hebrews defining faith at the start of the great 'Hall of Faith' chapter.
verses.kjv: Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.
verses.esv: Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen.

verses.context_note: James, the brother of Jesus, teaching about asking God with unwavering faith.
verses.kjv: But let him ask in faith, nothing wavering. For he that wavereth is like a wave of the sea driven with the wind and tossed.
verses.esv: But let him ask in faith, with no doubting, for the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea that is driven and tossed by the wind.

verses.context_note: Paul explaining to the church in Rome how faith is produced.
verses.kjv: So then faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.
verses.esv: So faith comes from hearing, and hearing through the word of Christ.

verses.context_note: Jesus catching Peter as he sinks while walking on water, questioning his doubt.
verses.kjv: And immediately Jesus stretched forth his hand, and caught him, and said unto him, O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?
verses.esv: Jesus immediately reached out his hand and took hold of him, saying to him, "O you of little faith, why did you doubt?"

verses.context_note: The risen Jesus appearing to doubting Thomas, inviting him to believe.
verses.kjv: Then saith he to Thomas, Reach hither thy finger, and behold my hands; and reach hither thy hand, and thrust it into my side: and be not faithless, but believing. And Thomas answered and said unto him, My Lord and my God. Jesus saith unto him, Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.
verses.esv: Then he said to Thomas, "Put your finger here, and see my hands; and put out your hand, and place it in my side. Do not disbelieve, but believe." Thomas answered him, "My Lord and my God!" Jesus said to him, "Have you believed because you have seen me? Blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed."

verses.context_note: A psalm of the sons of Korah, calling believers to stillness and trust in God's sovereignty.
verses.kjv: Be still, and know that I am God: I will be exalted among the heathen, I will be exalted in the earth.
verses.esv: Be still, and know that I am God. I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth!

verses.context_note: Isaiah encouraging the weary exiles to wait on the Lord for renewed strength.
verses.kjv: But they that wait upon the LORD shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles; they shall run, and not be weary; and they shall walk, and not faint.
verses.esv: But they who wait for the LORD shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings like eagles; they shall run and not be weary; they shall walk and not faint.

verses.context_note: Solomon on trusting the Lord completely
verses.kjv: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding. In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.
verses.esv: Trust in the LORD with all your heart, and do not lean on your own understanding. In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths.

verses.context_note: Jesus teaching his disciples about the power of faith
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Because of your unbelief: for verily I say unto you, If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say unto this mountain, Remove hence to yonder place; and it shall remove; and nothing shall be impossible unto you.
verses.esv: He said to them, "Because of your little faith. For truly, I say to you, if you have faith like a grain of mustard seed, you will say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move, and nothing will be impossible for you."

verses.context_note: The angel Gabriel speaking to Mary
verses.kjv: For with God nothing shall be impossible.
verses.esv: For nothing will be impossible with God.

verses.context_note: Paul on walking by faith to the church in Corinth
verses.kjv: (For we walk by faith, not by sight:)
verses.esv: For we walk by faith, not by sight.

verses.context_note: The author of Hebrews on the necessity of faith
verses.kjv: But without faith it is impossible to please him: for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him.
verses.esv: And without faith it is impossible to please him, for whoever would draw near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards those who seek him.

verses.context_note: Solomon contrasting the sluggard's desires with the diligent person's reward.
verses.kjv: The soul of the sluggard desireth, and hath nothing: but the soul of the diligent shall be made fat.
verses.esv: The soul of the sluggard craves and gets nothing, while the soul of the diligent is richly supplied.

verses.context_note: Paul instructing the Colossians to work wholeheartedly as unto the Lord.
verses.kjv: And whatsoever ye do, do it heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men;
verses.esv: Whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord and not for men,

verses.context_note: Solomon using the ant as an example of self-motivated diligence.
verses.kjv: Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise: Which having no guide, overseer, or ruler, Provideth her meat in the summer, and gathereth her food in the harvest.
verses.esv: Go to the ant, O sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise. Without having any chief, officer, or ruler, she prepares her bread in summer and gathers her food in harvest.

verses.context_note: Paul addressing idleness in the church in Thessalonica while they awaited Christ's return.
verses.kjv: For even when we were with you, this we commanded you, that if any would not work, neither should he eat.
verses.esv: For even when we were with you, we would give you this command: If anyone is not willing to work, let him not eat.

verses.context_note: The Preacher urging readers to work with full effort in their allotted time.
verses.kjv: Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do, do it with thy might; for there is no work, nor device, nor knowledge, nor wisdom, in the grave, whither thou goest.
verses.esv: Whatever your hand finds to do, do it with your might, for there is no work or thought or knowledge or wisdom in Sheol, to which you are going.

verses.context_note: Solomon's wisdom connecting diligence to leadership and laziness to servitude.
verses.kjv: The hand of the diligent shall bear rule: but the slothful shall be under tribute.
verses.esv: The hand of the diligent will rule, while the slothful will be put to forced labor.

verses.context_note: The author of Hebrews encouraging believers to imitate those who inherit the promises.
verses.kjv: That ye be not slothful, but followers of them who through faith and patience inherit the promises.
verses.esv: So that you may not be sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and patience inherit the promises.

verses.context_note: Paul encouraging the Galatian believers to persevere in doing good.
verses.kjv: And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not.
verses.esv: And let us not grow weary of doing good, for in due season we will reap, if we do not give up.

verses.context_note: Solomon contrasting lazy and diligent hands
verses.kjv: He becometh poor that dealeth with a slack hand: but the hand of the diligent maketh rich.
verses.esv: A slack hand causes poverty, but the hand of the diligent makes rich.

verses.context_note: Solomon on the sluggard who does not plow in season
verses.kjv: The sluggard will not plow by reason of the cold; therefore shall he beg in harvest, and have nothing.
verses.esv: The sluggard does not plow in the autumn; he will seek at harvest and have nothing.

verses.context_note: Paul urging the church in Rome to be fervent in spirit
verses.kjv: Not slothful in business; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord;
verses.esv: Do not be slothful in zeal, be fervent in spirit, serve the Lord.

verses.context_note: Paul encouraging steadfastness and abundance in the Lord's work
verses.kjv: Therefore, my beloved brethren, be ye stedfast, unmoveable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, forasmuch as ye know that your labour is not in vain in the Lord.
verses.esv: Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that in the Lord your labor is not in vain.

verses.context_note: Solomon on the kinship between sloth and destruction
verses.kjv: He also that is slothful in his work is brother to him that is a great waster.
verses.esv: Whoever is slack in his work is a brother to him who destroys.

verses.context_note: Solomon's wisdom on finding a wife as a gift from the Lord.
verses.kjv: Whoso findeth a wife findeth a good thing, and obtaineth favour of the LORD.
verses.esv: He who finds a wife finds a good thing and obtains favor from the LORD.

verses.context_note: The words of King Lemuel's mother describing the virtuous wife.
verses.kjv: Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies. The heart of her husband doth safely trust in her, so that he shall have no need of spoil.
verses.esv: An excellent wife who can find? She is far more precious than jewels. The heart of her husband trusts in her, and he will have no lack of gain.

verses.context_note: God's declaration in the Garden of Eden that man should not be alone.
verses.kjv: And the LORD God said, It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him an help meet for him.
verses.esv: Then the LORD God said, "It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a helper fit for him."

verses.context_note: Paul warning the Corinthian believers about being unequally partnered with unbelievers.
verses.kjv: Be ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers: for what fellowship hath righteousness with unrighteousness? and what communion hath light with darkness?
verses.esv: Do not be unequally yoked with unbelievers. For what partnership has righteousness with lawlessness? Or what fellowship has light with darkness?

verses.context_note: Solomon noting that a prudent wife is a special gift from the Lord.
verses.kjv: House and riches are the inheritance of fathers: and a prudent wife is from the LORD.
verses.esv: House and wealth are inherited from fathers, but a prudent wife is from the LORD.

verses.context_note: Paul's instruction to the Corinthians about marriage to fellow believers.
verses.kjv: The wife is bound by the law as long as her husband liveth; but if her husband be dead, she is at liberty to be married to whom she will; only in the Lord.
verses.esv: A wife is bound to her husband as long as he lives. But if her husband dies, she is free to be married to whom she wishes, only in the Lord.

verses.context_note: Paul teaching the church in Ephesus about the husband's sacrificial love for his wife.
verses.kjv: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;
verses.esv: Husbands, love your wives, as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her,

verses.context_note: The conclusion of the virtuous woman passage, prioritizing godly character.
verses.kjv: Favour is deceitful, and beauty is vain: but a woman that feareth the LORD, she shall be praised.
verses.esv: Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain, but a woman who fears the LORD is to be praised.

verses.context_note: Ruth's declaration of loyalty to Naomi, her mother-in-law
verses.kjv: And Ruth said, Intreat me not to leave thee, or to return from following after thee: for whither thou goest, I will go; and where thou lodgest, I will lodge: thy people shall be my people, and thy God my God: Where thou diest, will I die, and there will I be buried: the LORD do so to me, and more also, if ought but death part thee and me.
verses.esv: But Ruth said, "Do not urge me to leave you or to return from following you. For where you go I will go, and where you lodge I will lodge. Your people shall be my people, and your God my God. Where you die I will die, and there will I be buried. May the LORD do so to me and more also if anything but death parts me from you."

verses.context_note: Solomon on the strength of companionship
verses.kjv: Two are better than one; because they have a good reward for their labour. For if they fall, the one will lift up his fellow: but woe to him that is alone when he falleth; for he hath not another to help him up. Again, if two lie together, then they have heat: but how can one be warm alone? And if one prevail against him, two shall withstand him; and a threefold cord is not quickly broken.
verses.esv: Two are better than one, because they have a good reward for their toil. For if they fall, one will lift up his fellow. But woe to him who is alone when he falls and has not another to lift him up! Again, if two lie together, they keep warm, but how can one keep warm alone? And though a man might prevail against one who is alone, two will withstand him--a threefold cord is not quickly broken.

verses.context_note: Peter instructing husbands on honoring their wives
verses.kjv: Likewise, ye husbands, dwell with them according to knowledge, giving honour unto the wife, as unto the weaker vessel, and as being heirs together of the grace of life; that your prayers be not hindered.
verses.esv: Likewise, husbands, live with your wives in an understanding way, showing honor to the woman as the weaker vessel, since they are heirs with you of the grace of life, so that your prayers may not be hindered.

verses.context_note: The author of Hebrews on the honor of marriage
verses.kjv: Marriage is honourable in all, and the bed undefiled: but whoremongers and adulterers God will judge.
verses.esv: Let marriage be held in honor among all, and let the marriage bed be undefiled, for God will judge the sexually immoral and adulterous.

verses.context_note: Amos the prophet on the necessity of agreement
verses.kjv: Can two walk together, except they be agreed?
verses.esv: Do two walk together, unless they have agreed to meet?

verses.context_note: Paul instructing husbands and wives at Colossae
verses.kjv: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as it is fit in the Lord. Husbands, love your wives, and be not bitter against them.
verses.esv: Wives, submit to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord. Husbands, love your wives, and do not be harsh with them.

verses.context_note: Solomon's wisdom about preparing properly before building your house.
verses.kjv: Prepare thy work without, and make it fit for thyself in the field; and afterwards build thine house.
verses.esv: Prepare your work outside; get everything ready for yourself in the field, and after that build your house.

verses.context_note: A psalm of Solomon about the futility of building without the Lord's blessing.
verses.kjv: Except the LORD build the house, they labour in vain that build it: except the LORD keep the city, the watchman waketh but in vain.
verses.esv: Unless the LORD builds the house, those who build it labor in vain. Unless the LORD watches over the city, the watchman stays awake in vain.

verses.context_note: Solomon's wisdom on the value of careful planning versus hasty decisions.
verses.kjv: The thoughts of the diligent tend only to plenteousness; but of every one that is hasty only to want.
verses.esv: The plans of the diligent lead surely to abundance, but everyone who is hasty comes only to poverty.

verses.context_note: Jeremiah's letter to the the exiles of Judah in Babylon, instructing them to settle and build.
verses.kjv: Build ye houses, and dwell in them; and plant gardens, and eat the fruit of them; Take ye wives, and beget sons and daughters; and take wives for your sons, and give your daughters to husbands, that they may bear sons and daughters; that ye may be increased there, and not diminished. And seek the peace of the city whither I have caused you to be carried away captives, and pray unto the LORD for it: for in the peace thereof shall ye have peace.
verses.esv: Build houses and live in them; plant gardens and eat their produce. Take wives and have sons and daughters; take wives for your sons, and give your daughters in marriage, that they may bear sons and daughters; multiply there, and do not decrease. But seek the welfare of the city where I have sent you into exile, and pray to the LORD on its behalf, for in its welfare you will find your welfare.

verses.context_note: The prophet Haggai challenging the returned exiles who built their own homes but neglected God's temple.
verses.kjv: Is it time for you, O ye, to dwell in your cieled houses, and this house lie waste? Now therefore thus saith the LORD of hosts; Consider your ways. Ye have sown much, and bring in little; ye eat, but ye have not enough; ye drink, but ye are not filled with drink; ye clothe you, but there is none warm; and he that earneth wages earneth wages to put it into a bag with holes.
verses.esv: Is it a time for you yourselves to dwell in your paneled houses, while this house lies in ruins? Now, therefore, thus says the LORD of hosts: Consider your ways. You have sown much, and harvested little. You eat, but you never have enough; you drink, but you never have your fill. You clothe yourselves, but no one is warm. And he who earns wages does so to put them into a bag with holes.

verses.context_note: Solomon's wisdom about honest gain and avoiding greed in financial matters.
verses.kjv: He that is greedy of gain troubleth his own house; but he that hateth gifts shall live.
verses.esv: Whoever is greedy for unjust gain troubles his own household, but he who hates bribes will live.

verses.context_note: Jesus teaching about counting the cost, using a building illustration.
verses.kjv: For which of you, intending to build a tower, sitteth not down first, and counteth the cost, whether he have sufficient to finish it? Lest haply, after he hath laid the foundation, and is not able to finish it, all that behold it begin to mock him, Saying, This man began to build, and was not able to finish.
verses.esv: For which of you, desiring to build a tower, does not first sit down and count the cost, whether he has enough to complete it? Otherwise, when he has laid a foundation and is not able to finish, all who see it begin to mock him, saying, 'This man began to build and was not able to finish.'

verses.context_note: Solomon contrasting God's curse on the wicked with His blessing on the righteous home.
verses.kjv: The curse of the LORD is in the house of the wicked: but he blesseth the habitation of the just.
verses.esv: The LORD's curse is on the house of the wicked, but he blesses the dwelling of the righteous.

verses.context_note: Solomon contrasting the wicked and the righteous household
verses.kjv: The wicked are overthrown, and are not: but the house of the righteous shall stand.
verses.esv: The wicked are overthrown and are no more, but the house of the righteous will stand.

verses.context_note: Isaiah prophesying a time of peace and secure dwellings
verses.kjv: And my people shall dwell in a peaceable habitation, and in sure dwellings, and in quiet resting places;
verses.esv: My people will abide in a peaceful habitation, in secure dwellings, and in quiet resting places.

verses.context_note: Moses declaring blessings for obedience
verses.kjv: Blessed shalt thou be when thou comest in, and blessed shalt thou be when thou goest out.
verses.esv: Blessed shall you be when you come in, and blessed shall you be when you go out.

verses.context_note: Solomon on the wise woman who builds her house
verses.kjv: Every wise woman buildeth her house: but the foolish plucketh it down with her hands.
verses.esv: The wisest of women builds her house, but folly with her own hands tears it down.

verses.context_note: Jesus teaching about building on a firm foundation
verses.kjv: Therefore whosoever heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them, I will liken him unto a wise man, which built his house upon a rock: And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell not: for it was founded upon a rock.
verses.esv: Everyone then who hears these words of mine and does them will be like a wise man who built his house on the rock. And the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house, but it did not fall, because it had been founded on the rock.

verses.context_note: Solomon's wisdom about committing one's plans to the Lord.
verses.kjv: Commit thy works unto the LORD, and thy thoughts shall be established.
verses.esv: Commit your work to the LORD, and your plans will be established.

verses.context_note: God's promise through Jeremiah to the the exiles of Judah in Babylon about their future.
verses.kjv: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, saith the LORD, thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you an expected end.
verses.esv: For I know the plans I have for you, declares the LORD, plans for welfare and not for evil, to give you a future and a hope.

verses.context_note: Paul instructing the Colossians to work as unto the Lord in all they do.
verses.kjv: And whatsoever ye do, do it heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men; Knowing that of the Lord ye shall receive the reward of the inheritance: for ye serve the Lord Christ.
verses.esv: Whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord and not for men, knowing that from the Lord you will receive the inheritance as your reward. You are serving the Lord Christ.

verses.context_note: Solomon's instruction to trust fully in the Lord rather than one's own understanding.
verses.kjv: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding. In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.
verses.esv: Trust in the LORD with all your heart, and do not lean on your own understanding. In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths.

verses.context_note: David's psalm encouraging believers to delight in the Lord and trust His direction.
verses.kjv: Delight thyself also in the LORD; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart. Commit thy way unto the LORD; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.
verses.esv: Delight yourself in the LORD, and he will give you the desires of your heart. Commit your way to the LORD; trust in him, and he will act.

verses.context_note: The Preacher urging readers to give their full effort to their work.
verses.kjv: Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do, do it with thy might; for there is no work, nor device, nor knowledge, nor wisdom, in the grave, whither thou goest.
verses.esv: Whatever your hand finds to do, do it with your might, for there is no work or thought or knowledge or wisdom in Sheol, to which you are going.

verses.context_note: Paul writing to the Ephesians about God's purpose in creating believers for good works.
verses.kjv: For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works, which God hath before ordained that we should walk in them.
verses.esv: For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand, that we should walk in them.

verses.context_note: Paul teaching the church in Rome about using their different gifts faithfully.
verses.kjv: Having then gifts differing according to the grace that is given to us, whether prophecy, let us prophesy according to the proportion of faith; Or ministry, let us wait on our ministering: or he that teacheth, on teaching; Or he that exhorteth, on exhortation: he that giveth, let him do it with simplicity; he that ruleth, with diligence; he that sheweth mercy, with cheerfulness.
verses.esv: Having gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, let us use them: if prophecy, in proportion to our faith; if service, in our serving; the one who teaches, in his teaching; the one who exhorts, in his exhortation; the one who contributes, in generosity; the one who leads, with zeal; the one who does acts of mercy, with cheerfulness.

verses.context_note: Moses praying for God's favor on the work of Israel's hands
verses.kjv: And let the beauty of the LORD our God be upon us: and establish thou the work of our hands upon us; yea, the work of our hands establish thou it.
verses.esv: Let the favor of the Lord our God be upon us, and establish the work of our hands upon us; yes, establish the work of our hands!

verses.context_note: Solomon on the value of honest labor
verses.kjv: He that tilleth his land shall be satisfied with bread: but he that followeth vain persons is void of understanding.
verses.esv: Whoever works his land will have plenty of bread, but he who follows worthless pursuits lacks sense.

verses.context_note: Solomon on the reward of skillful work
